title: Digital Signatures with Ed25519 and ECDSA · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed security review reconstructs digital signatures with ed25519 and ecdsa from threat, algorithm, parameter, implementation, key-custody, failure, test-vector, operations, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for versioned signed artifact verifier after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State exactly what a valid signature proves about bytes, key control, and context—and what it does not prove about truth, time, authorization, or intent.
Generate, parse, sign, and verify Ed25519 keys and signatures with exact message/context rules and trusted key selection.
Use ECDSA through current Go APIs with approved curves, supported hashes, canonical encodings, and no custom nonce generation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Crypto Go Signature Guarantees · changed case · Review Crypto Go Signature Guar…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review cryptographic engineering team handles case 7664 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify canonical context-bound Ed25519 or ECDSA bytes under a trusted key and separate authorization policy; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State exactly what a valid signature proves about bytes, key control, and context—and what it does not prove about truth, time, authorization, or intent.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Apply threshold, authorization, revocation, expiry, transparency, and audit policy after cryptographic verification.

hints: For Cryptography and Go Record what required effort · Review Crypto Go Signature Policy, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Crypto Go Signature Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Cryptography and Go Record what required effort · Review Crypto Go Signature Policy, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review cryptographic engineering team handles case 9997 during a certificate rollover incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify canonical context-bound Ed25519 or ECDSA bytes under a trusted key and separate authorization policy; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply threshold, authorization, revocation, expiry, transparency, and audit policy after cryptographic verification.

For Cryptography and Go Record what required effort · Review Crypto Go Signature Policy, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Cryptographic verification is the final authorization decision.
